recentEmails.subject: Updated Call Schedule for March
recentEmails.preview: Hi Maya, attaching the updated overnight call schedule for the remainder of March. Please review the changes to your ICU coverage...
recentEmails.body:
Hi Maya, attaching the updated overnight call schedule for the remainder of March. Please review the changes to your ICU coverage and night shifts.

Attached is the full overnight call schedule for the remainder of March, including specific adjustments to your ICU coverage, particularly on the weekends of the 14th and 21st where we had to modify staffing.

If any of the updates create significant hardship for you, reply to this email by Thursday so we can see if trades are possible within your class.

Best,
Residency Program Office
UT Health Austin

recentEmails.subject: Dinner when you come to Houston?
recentEmails.preview: Hi beta, your dad and I were thinking maybe we cook your favorite dal when you visit next weekend. Let us know if you are still coming...
recentEmails.body:
Hi beta, your dad and I were thinking maybe we cook your favorite dal when you visit next weekend. Let us know if you are still coming so we can plan dinner.

Your dad and I know you have been working very hard and want you to relax a little at home.

Tell us if you are still coming on Saturday night and whether you will stay until Monday morning. Also let me know if you are craving anything else so I can get the ingredients in advance.

We are very proud of you.

Love,
Mom

recentEmails.subject: New Clinical Article: Advances in Heart Failure Treatment
recentEmails.preview: A new article relevant to internal medicine was published today. Based on your specialty we thought you might find this useful...
recentEmails.body:
Hi Dr. Patel,

A new article relevant to internal medicine was published today, and based on your specialty we thought you might find this useful.

The article, "Advances in Heart Failure Treatment for Hospitalized Patients," reviews updated guideline-directed medical therapy, practical titration strategies on the wards, and emerging therapies for patients with reduced ejection fraction. It also includes brief case vignettes and a summary table you can reference on rounds.

Click through to read the full article and save it to your library if you want to refer back to it later.

Best,
Doximity Alerts

recentEmails.subject: Rent Payment Confirmation
recentEmails.preview: Hello Maya, this is a confirmation that we successfully received your rent payment for March. Thank you for your prompt payment...
recentEmails.body:
Hello Maya, this is a confirmation that we successfully received your rent payment for March. Thank you for your prompt payment.

Your payment of $1,650 was processed on March 3 and has been applied to your account.

No further action is required at this time. If you believe this amount is incorrect or you have questions about future payments, please reply to this email or call the leasing office during business hours.

Thank you for being a valued resident at Riverside Apartments.

Best regards,
Riverside Apartments Leasing Office

recentEmails.subject: Case Follow-up from Yesterday
recentEmails.preview: Maya, good work presenting the case during rounds. I wanted to send you the guideline update we discussed regarding management...
recentEmails.body:
Maya, good work presenting the case during rounds. I wanted to send you the guideline update we discussed regarding management of a recent case.

I’m attaching the guideline update on management of acute decompensated heart failure in the setting of chronic kidney disease.

Take a look at the attached PDF and focus on the sections around diuretic dosing, monitoring urine output, and avoiding nephrotoxic medications. These are the areas I think will come up again on your next rotation.

If you have questions after reading, feel free to grab me after sign-out or send a quick reply here.

Best,
Michael

recentEmails.subject: Your Apple Watch Weekly Activity Summary
recentEmails.preview: Here’s your weekly activity summary. You closed your exercise ring three times this week. Keep up the good work...
recentEmails.body:
Here’s your weekly activity summary. You closed your Exercise ring three times this week—keep up the good work!

You also met your Move goal on four days. Your average daily steps were slightly lower than last week, but your standing hours improved.

Highlights:
- Longest workout: 38 minutes on Tuesday evening
- Most active day: Saturday
- Best standing streak: 13 hours on Wednesday

Consider scheduling short walks on your lighter call days to help maintain your streaks.

— Apple Watch Activity

recentEmails.subject: Coffee this weekend?
recentEmails.preview: Hey Maya, I know residency has been crazy but if you have a free hour this weekend we should grab coffee near campus...
recentEmails.body:
Hey Maya, I know residency has been crazy but if you have a free hour this weekend we should grab coffee near campus.

There’s a new place that opened on Riverside that has good reviews and lots of quiet corners.

Totally understand if you’re on call or need to sleep, but if Sunday afternoon works even for 45 minutes it would be great to catch up and hear how things are going.

Text me your call schedule and we’ll figure something out.

— Alex

recentEmails.subject: Student Loan Payment Reminder
recentEmails.preview: This is a reminder that your upcoming student loan payment is scheduled for March 15. Please review your repayment plan...
recentEmails.body:
This is a reminder that your upcoming student loan payment is scheduled for March 15. Please review your repayment plan to make sure everything looks correct.

Your current monthly payment amount is $420.17 under your income-driven repayment plan. Please ensure that your designated bank account has sufficient funds available at least one business day before the scheduled debit.

If your income or employment status has recently changed, you may be eligible to recertify your income and adjust your monthly payment. Visit your loan servicer portal for details.

Thank you,
Federal Student Aid

recentEmails.subject: Your Class Reservation
recentEmails.preview: Hi Maya, your spot for the Saturday evening restorative yoga session has been confirmed. Looking forward to seeing you...
recentEmails.body:
Hi Maya, your spot for the Saturday evening restorative yoga session has been confirmed, and we’re looking forward to seeing you.

Class will begin at 7:00 PM and doors open 15 minutes early.

Please bring a water bottle and wear comfortable layers. Mats and props will be provided, but you’re welcome to bring your own if you prefer.

If you need to cancel or reschedule, reply to this email at least 4 hours before class time.

— Downtown Yoga Studio

recentEmails.subject: Morning Medical News Roundup
recentEmails.preview: Top medical headlines today include updates in diabetes management and new residency wellness research...
recentEmails.body:
Good morning,

Top medical headlines today include updates in diabetes management and new residency wellness research, plus a brief summary of recent policy changes affecting inpatient reimbursement.

Several articles may be particularly relevant for internal medicine residents, including a quick-read piece on managing overnight hyperglycemia and a podcast episode on coping strategies during demanding rotations.

Scroll through to read the full stories or save them to your list for later.

— Medscape Daily Brief
